subject: Policy-Dynamic URLs for PDPs
commit_message:
Policy-Dynamic URLs for PDPs

Issue-ID: OOM-850

Change-Id: I1dcd58f1b0fb2e802c08954e23c551def2588849
Signed-off-by: Durgpal <durgpal@amdocs.com>

Patch Set 1:

post upgrading replica count of PDP to 3, below is the configmap for pap.conf with updated URLs-  

pap.conf:
----

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

# pap component installation configuration parameters

# tomcat specific parameters

TOMCAT_JMX_PORT=9990
TOMCAT_SHUTDOWN_PORT=9405
SSL_HTTP_CONNECTOR_PORT=9091
SSL_AJP_CONNECTOR_PORT=8380
SSL_AJP_CONNECTOR_REDIRECT_PORT=8443

TOMCAT_X_MS_MB=1024
TOMCAT_X_MX_MB=1024

# pap properties

PAP_PDPS=/opt/app/policy/servers/pap/bin/pdps
PAP_URL=http://pap:9091/pap/

PAP_INITIATE_PDP=true
PAP_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL=10000
PAP_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT=10000

REST_ADMIN_DOMAIN=com
REST_ADMIN_REPOSITORY=repository
REST_ADMIN_WORKSPACE=workspace

# PDP related properties
PAP_PDP_URL= http://dp2018-pdp-0.pdp.casdp.svc.cluster.local:8081/pdp/,testpdp,alpha123; http://dp2018-pdp-1.pdp.casdp.svc.cluster.local:8081/pdp/,testpdp,alpha123; http://dp2018-pdp-2.pdp.casdp.svc.cluster.local:8081/pdp/,testpdp,alpha123;
PAP_PDP_HTTP_USER_ID=testpdp
PAP_PDP_HTTP_PASSWORD=alpha123

Patch Set 1:

Hello Durgpal, the helm changes are very clever, but I have a major concern with the runtime behavior.  Let me know if I have misunderstood something about it.

So, if I understand correctly the changes, when a new pdp is grown, it will cause a reconfiguration of the pap, taking a healthy pap down with it (not sure if there may be other components).   This in turn, will trigger synchronization between policy components and will introduce at least temporary instability.  If my assumption is correct, the scale out operation would have the opposite effect, it will reduce availability of the system.   This is in contrast with the current way of operation, where if a pdp is scaled out, it will not take down the pap, or any other healthy components.

I agree that this existing helm code in the pap.conf is sub-optimal, but I am afraid that  the only reasonable solution is to modify the whitelisting functionality in the java code instead, after that, we can remove this piece of configuration from the helm charts.

Let me know your thoughts.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Folks, I need Jorge to be on-board with this before merging these changes. He will be temporarily unavailable until the 31st. Can we abandon or hold this off until he comes back?

Patch Set 1:

Is this patch being reformed to address concerns or should we abandon?

Patch Set 1:

As Pamela confirmed waiting for Jorge to be on-board. Let's wait for him to confirm till 31'st and if this is not needed we can abandon it before code freeze.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Yes, I think we should not merge this code.  It is very elegant but the side effect of taking the PAP down and the subsequent instability introduced when scaling out a PDP-X, I believe it to be severe enough to discourage the merge of this code.

I think the best solution to the issue that OOM-850 reports, is to do equivalent functionality in the policy side, PAP java code to avoid system disruption.   I created a task on the policy side to track it:

https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-1087

After that is done, the conservative bulk preprovisioning in the oom side in kubernetes/policy/resources/config/pe/pap.conf, could be removed.

Abandoned

Abandoning as recommended by Jorge.
